mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes subproject that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and operation of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to manage clusters across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which will manage the lifecycle of your clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure you are installing in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Cluster API cluster using a sample configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/example-cluster.yaml

```

> Replace with your custom cluster configuration as needed.

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all associated clusters and data.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command will remove the installed components.
> This command will remove the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backups of your clusters. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Drain all clusters before upgrading.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to install CRDs: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRDs are already installed or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check if the CRDs are already present using 'kubectl get crd' and ensure network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig and ensure the Kubernetes cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to misconfiguration in the Cluster API components.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the required CRDs are not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the provided YAML file.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the stability of existing components.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API versions for cluster management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your existing cluster configurations to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Cluster API components running in the kube-system namespace with 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system'. All pods should be in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
